# LONG TEXT:    You can write a full paragraph in any value cell. Wrap it in double

# COMMENTS:     Any row whose key starts with # is ignored by the site.
# Add as many images as you like: image_1  image_2  image_3  …

#   image_N_placement  →  full | wide | center | left | right

key: # Add as many paragraphs as you need: bio_1  bio_2  bio_3  …
# Each value becomes one paragraph of text on the Bio page.
# To remove a paragraph  delete its row or leave the value empty.

value: Roman Chystakhovskyi (b. 1997, Ukraine) is a sculptor based in Leipzig, Germany. Trained as a classical painter in Kyiv and forged through eight years as a senior digital sculptor for international studios, he now makes wall-mounted and freestanding sculptures of hands pressing into faces — capturing emotional states that have no clean name.

He cannot leave the moment when a feeling becomes physical: the hands that simultaneously comfort and crush, the face that cannot be read as one thing. Each work begins with a single line of music or poetry that unlocks something — he chases the image before it disappears, sculpts it digitally, then forces it into reality through 3D printing, hand-sculpting, silicone molding and gypsum casting. The friction between the weightless digital and the heavy physical is part of what the work is about.
Nothing is resolved. That's the point.
